key_insight: Curvature-Weighted Gradient Diversity (CWGD) reweights gradient noise by inverse curvature, giving a more geometry-aware measure of SGD noise than plain variance.
engineering_relevance: This enables learning-rate schedules that adapt to the loss landscape, improving convergence efficiency and final error in optimization-heavy machine learning systems.
method: Define CWGD as per-sample gradient diversity weighted by the inverse square root of the Hessian.
Use a Hutchinson-based diagonal Hessian estimator, which is exact for quadratic objectives.
Modulate cosine annealing with CWGD to reduce the effective optimization noise floor.
evidence_signal: In quadratic settings across varying condition numbers, batch sizes, and noise structures, CWGD-Cosine achieved about 20% lower final optimization error and up to a 2x reduction in asymptotic error floor versus standard cosine annealing.
watch_item: The approach depends on Hessian estimates that can become stale or less reliable in non-convex training.
paper_title: Curvature-Weighted Gradient Diversity: A Noise Measure for Geometry-Adaptive SGD Schedules
